prompt: Paper-collage stop-motion assembly. Use Image 1 as the exact empty first frame (a flat burnt-orange paper field) and Image 2 as the exact completed last frame. One continuous locked-off vertical shot, no camera movement, PACED EVENLY ACROSS THE ENTIRE FIVE SECONDS: pieces keep arriving continuously through the whole shot, do NOT finish early and do NOT hold a long static frame. Open on the empty burnt-orange field; the balance-scale central post and beam slide in and lock, the two pans drop onto their chains, then stacks of halftone document folders pile onto the left pan one after another tilting it downward while a single large speech-bubble settles onto the right pan, and finally the wooden gavel snaps into place at lower right in the last second to complete Image 2. Preserve the 9:16 framing, cream keylines, brick-red cardstock accents, uncoated paper grain, halftone dots, crisp cut edges and soft shadows. Restrained tactile 2D paper craft only. No scene cuts, no camera movement, no zoom, no morphing, no new objects, no text, no letters, no numbers, no logos, no watermark, no UI, no sound.
